title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a freshman, accused Masconomet Regional High School of inadequate response to harassment, including threats of sexual violence.
Teachers at Masconomet allege administrators ignored incidents of gender discrimination and failed to act on retaliation threats.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes claims of inaction, citing privacy rules, but emphasizes the district's procedures for handling misconduct.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 billion extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers have paid over $1 billion extra for gas since the Iran War began in late February.
The average cost to fill a Ford F-150 increased by $37, now totaling $145 per tank.
Massachusetts home heating costs rose 37.9%, increasing expenses by $224 per oil tank since March 2025.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Canoe River Campground in Mansfield offers nearly 300 sites, starting at $51 per night, during World Cup matches.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver has glamping cabins and RV sites, starting at $140 per night.
Boston hotels are raising prices for World Cup, while FIFA reduced hotel bookings due to inflated estimates.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, a stroke survivor, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke in 2019, resulting in paralysis of the left side of his body.
Rafferty previously completed the Boston Marathon in 2021, two years after his stroke recovery at Spaulding.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden for the first time since his 2025 injury during a Celtics-Knicks game.
Tatum appreciated the applause from Knicks fans despite pregame nerves; he played 40 minutes in Boston's 112-106 loss.
Tatum views Madison Square Garden as significant to his career, marking both a low point and a place of personal growth.

title: Everett woman arrested after dog attack injures pet and owner
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 49-year-old woman was arrested in Everett after her dog allegedly attacked another dog and its owner.
The woman was identified through video surveillance and arrested for outstanding warrants on Ferry Street.
The 11-month-old hound involved in the attack will be quarantined by Everett Animal Control.

title: Wife of Maine father who died saving his children opens up about their ‘love story’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Ryan Jennings, 46, drowned on April 1 saving his children from a riptide near Jupiter Beach in Florida.
Two GoFundMe campaigns have raised over $240,000 combined for the Jennings family following Ryan's death.
Emily Jennings shared their love story, highlighting Ryan's recovery and dedication to helping others overcome addiction.

title: A Vermont ski lift is being retired after six decades
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Pico Mountain's Outpost Double chairlift in Vermont, installed in 1969, is retired after nearly 60 years due to mechanical failures.
A main drive shaft failure led to the decision, with officials citing the lift as the last of its kind in North America.
Temporary solutions are being considered as replacement efforts face delays; the Outpost chairs will be auctioned this summer.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge on recent trends in Greater Boston.
Peter Chianca is Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
Chianca has extensive experience as a news editor, columnist, and music writer in Greater Boston.

title: Jayson Tatum returns to MSG, but Knicks hold off Celtics late: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to MSG for the first time after his Achilles injury, scoring 24 points in a Celtics' 112-106 loss to the Knicks.
The Knicks lead the season series 3-0 over the Celtics, as Josh Hart scored 26 points with crucial late-game three-pointers.
Payton Pritchard contributed 23 points for the Celtics, but issues in defending Knicks' bigs Towns and Robinson persisted.
